item.block2: occurs when harmful effects result from the entry into the ocean of chemicals, particles, waste, or the spread of invasive organisms.
are waste material that pollute air, water or soil.
occurs when discharges, namely man-made contaminants, enter a water body at a single location.
is the water flow which occurs when soil is saturated and excess water overflows into the land.
when it is chemically harmful or even deadly.
are drifting organisms that inhabit the oceans or seas.
are organisms which live on, in, or near the seabed.
are animals that feed by straining suspended matter and food particles from water.
when animals take in more toxics than they excrete.
describe the eating relationships between living species within an ecosystem.
are semi-enclosed coastal bodies of water with one or more rivers or streams and with a free connection to the open sea.
refers to water that is depleted of dissolved oxygen.

item.block1: A pesticide is a substance or mixture of substances
item.block2: used to kill a chemical substance, biological agent, antimicrobial, disinfectant or device used against any pest.
include oceans, salt marshes, estuaries, lagoons, mangroves, coral reefs, the deep sea and the sea floor.
describe the eating relationships between living species within an ecosystem.
is the act or process of being altered or changed.
is an illness which affects a person, animal or plant. Diseases are defined as pathological conditions of a part, organ, or system...
is the structural material which forms animal or plant cells.
is the study of chemical processes in living organisms.
refers to fish carcasses.
